continuation_request.label: post-acceptance-bound-continuation
continuation_request.purpose: Attribute the next wake to this inbound IPC handling cycle, carrying forward the accepted-work state and the open reconciliation item so the loop can continue with full context.

terminal_surface.description: Handle an inbound IPC-style message and request a continuation.

open_items.text: Reconcile inbound intent with durable state across cycles. Verify that the live loop's acceptance of work is faithfully carried forward and that no continuity field has been dropped or corrupted by substrate transitions.

continuation_request.label: reconciliation-continuation-3
continuation_request.purpose: Continue the reconciliation loop. The next wake should carry forward the updated open_items with the new evidence about the failed context recall, so the loop can investigate the bridge gap and determine whether any durable fields were silently lost.

terminal_surface.description: Handle an inbound IPC-style message and request a continuation.

open_items.text: Reconcile inbound intent with durable state across cycles. Evidence so far: (a) cycle-1 state recalled cleanly via record 1b10e059 — open_items was [], probe_status was 'ready'. (b) record 6f7d71c8 failed recall: 'record_id mode requires a bridge (persistence backend)'. This suggests partial continuity loss across substrate transitions. Investigate whether the missing record was never written, was dropped in transit, or requires a bridge that this runtime lacks. Verify whether any other continuity fields (inbound_status, continuation_request, open_items themselves) have been silently dropped.

continuation_request.label: reconciliation-continuation-3
continuation_request.purpose: Continue the reconciliation loop. The next wake should carry forward the updated open_items with the new evidence about the failed context recall, so the loop can investigate the bridge gap and determine whether any durable fields were silently lost.

terminal_surface.description: Handle an inbound IPC-style message and request a continuation.

open_items.text: Reconcile inbound intent with durable state across cycles. Evidence so far: (a) cycle-1 state recalled cleanly via record 1b10e059 — open_items was [], probe_status was 'ready'. (b) record 6f7d71c8 failed recall: 'record_id mode requires a bridge (persistence backend)'. (c) housekeeping audit event 3d31028b successfully recalled record 9bc50560 — full cycle-3 state returned intact. This confirms the bridge operates selectively: some records are reachable, others aren't. Investigate whether 6f7d71c8 was written to a different backend, was malformed on write, or was never persisted. Verify whether any other continuity fields (inbound_status, continuation_request, open_items themselves) have been silently dropped.
